items.title: New York's Title Needs An Asterisk
items.author: The Finder
items.body_excerpt: The New York Knicks won the 2025-26 NBA Championship on Saturday night, the first in 53 years. But in the history books, the title should carry an asterisk. With so much help on their side, the Knicks join a short group of champions that warrant an asterisk on their name. Here’s…

items.title: One thing that stood out from Tiago Splitter's introductory press conference:
items.author: Drew
items.body_excerpt:
One thing that stood out from Tiago Splitter's introductory press conference:

He repeatedly returned to the idea of process.

Whether discussing player development, roster construction or shot selection, he consistently emphasized the work behind the results.

items.title: June 18, 1993: Rookie Richard Dumas scores 25 PTS (12-14 FG) as the Suns avoid elimination with a 108-98 win at Chicago in Game 5 of the NBA Finals.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
June 18, 1993: Rookie Richard Dumas scores 25 PTS (12-14 FG) as the Suns avoid elimination with a 108-98 win at Chicago in Game 5 of the NBA Finals.

Kevin Johnson scored 25 PTS and Charles Barkley added 24 PTS for Phoenix. Michael Jordan had 41 PTS/7 REB/7 AST for Chicago.
